name: tmdb-mcp
description: Search and look up movies, TV shows and people from The Movie Database (TMDB), with IMDb/Rotten Tomatoes/Metacritic ratings via OMDb.

tools.name: search_multi
tools.description: Search movies, TV and people at once.

tools.name: get_movies
tools.description: Compact movie card(s) by id (1-20), no full details.

tools.name: get_tv_shows
tools.description: Compact TV show card(s) by id (1-20), no full details.

tools.name: discover_movies
tools.description: Find movies by filters (genre, year, rating, …).

tools.name: search_keywords
tools.description: Resolve keyword names to ids for discover.

tools.name: search_companies
tools.description: Resolve a production company name to its id.

tools.name: find_by_imdb_id
tools.description: Resolve an IMDb id to TMDB entities.

tools.name: get_ratings
tools.description: IMDb/RT/Metacritic ratings by IMDb id or title.

tools.name: get_similar
tools.description: Titles similar to a movie or TV show.

tools.name: get_reviews
tools.description: User reviews for a movie or TV show.

tools.name: get_collection
tools.description: A movie collection/franchise and its parts.

prompts.name: recommend_similar
prompts.description: Plan a search for movies or TV shows similar to a given title, using TMDB's similarity/recommendation data (and discover as a fallback) rather than the model's own knowledge. Use this instead of calling get_similar/get_movie_recommendations yourself when you want the merged, ranked shortlist in one step; call those tools directly only if you already have a specific TMDB id and just need its raw candidate list.
prompts.text:
I liked "{{title}}". Recommend {{count}} similar movies or TV shows using the available tools — don't rely on your own memory for the candidate list or ratings.

1. Resolve "{{title}}" to a TMDB id: use search_multi (or search_movies/search_tv if the media type is already known) and pick the best match.
2. Call get_similar and get_movie_recommendations/get_tv_recommendations for that id and merge the candidates — weigh get_movie_recommendations as the stronger signal (it's behavioral/co-viewing data), and use get_similar's genre/keyword matches mainly to fill gaps, discarding any that don't actually fit thematically.
3. If that pool has fewer than {{count}} good candidates, broaden with discover_movies/discover_tv filtered to the same genres.
4. Once you've picked the best {{count}} ids, call get_movies/get_tv_shows with include_ratings=true for all of them in one call (not get_movie/get_tv per title) to get title, year and ratings together. Return each with a one-line reason it fits.

prompts.name: top_by_entity
prompts.description: Find the best-regarded movies/TV from a specific person (actor, director, composer, …) or production company/studio, optionally restricted to one genre — 'A24's top movies', 'Tarantino's best films', 'which of Joe Hisaishi's scores are for animated films'. Resolves the name to a TMDB id (search_people/search_companies) then ranks with discover_movies/discover_tv, filtering out low-vote noise; for a person's TV work specifically it goes through get_person_credits instead, since discover_tv can't filter by person at all. Use this instead of doing that yourself when you want the ranked shortlist in one step.
prompts.text:
Find the top {{count}} best-regarded titles from "{{name}}" using the available tools — don't rely on your own memory for the candidate list or ratings.

1. Resolve "{{name}}" to a TMDB id: use search_people if it's a person, search_companies if it's a company/studio. Names aren't always unique — search_companies in particular can return several unrelated companies sharing the exact same name in different countries (e.g. "A24" also matches unrelated companies literally named "A24" elsewhere) — for a person, disambiguate with known_for_department/popularity; for a company, with origin_country (prefer the one based where the studio actually is).
2. If a genre was given, resolve it to a genre id via get_movie_genres/get_tv_genres.
3. Unless media_type is 'tv': call discover_movies with with_people (person id — matches cast OR crew) or with_companies (company id), plus with_genres if a genre was given, sort_by=vote_average.desc, and a min_votes floor (e.g. 100-1000) to exclude single-vote noise.
4. Unless media_type is 'movie': if this is a COMPANY, discover_tv's with_companies works the same way as discover_movies's with_companies filter. If this is a PERSON, discover_tv can NOT filter by person at all — it doesn't even accept with_cast/with_crew/with_people as parameters for TV (a validation error, not a silent no-op) — instead call get_person_credits for that id, keep only entries with media_type 'tv', then batch-fetch ratings/genres via get_tv_shows and filter to the requested genre yourself if one was given.
5. Merge whichever of movies/TV apply, sort by rating, and return the top {{count}}: title/name, year, media type, and rating.

tmdb_api_token.title: TMDB API Read Access Token
tmdb_api_token.description: Required. TMDB v4 'Read Access Token' from https://www.themoviedb.org/settings/api (sent as a Bearer token).

omdb_api_key.title: OMDb API Key
omdb_api_key.description: Optional. Free key from https://www.omdbapi.com/apikey.aspx — enables IMDb/Rotten Tomatoes/Metacritic ratings.

tmdb_language.title: Language
tmdb_language.description: Optional. Default response language as ISO-639-1, optionally with a region — e.g. 'ru-RU', 'en-US', 'ja'. Localizes titles, overviews and genre names. Defaults to en-US.

tmdb_region.title: Region
tmdb_region.description: Optional. Default ISO-3166-1 country code (e.g. 'US', 'RU', 'GB') for region-specific results like certifications. Defaults to US.
